description: Fix three bugs in the MCP server's settings and dispatch handling: (1) a race condition where plugin settings are silently discarded when a fire-and-forget disk write races with an immediate config reload, (2) JSON-RPC response broadcasting that sends responses to all extension connections instead of only the requesting one, and (3) missing type validation for non-url settings fields in the HTTP /plugin-settings endpoint.

userStories.title: Fix settings-reload race in handleConfigSetPluginSettings
userStories.description: In handleConfigSetPluginSettings (extension-handlers.ts), the call to callbacks.onPluginSettingsPersist() is fire-and-forget and races with the immediately-awaited callbacks.onReload(). reloadCore reads settings from disk and overwrites state.pluginSettings, discarding the freshly-set in-memory settings if the disk write hasn't completed yet. Fix by awaiting the disk write before triggering the reload.
acceptanceCriteria: In handleConfigSetPluginSettings, savePluginSettings is awaited (not fire-and-forget) before callbacks.onReload() is called
The plugins.changed notification sent after reload contains the newly-saved settings, not the old values
callbacks.onPluginSettingsPersist() is either removed from this handler or converted to a no-op since the save is now explicit
userStories.notes:
Files:
- platform/mcp-server/src/extension-handlers.ts (await the save before onReload)
- platform/mcp-server/src/http-routes.ts (wire savePluginSettings reference if needed for the await)

Context:
In handleConfigSetPluginSettings (extension-handlers.ts around line 728-762), the current flow is:
  state.pluginSettings[pluginName] = settingsObj;  // line 734
  callbacks.onPluginSettingsPersist();  // fire-and-forget disk write
  try { await callbacks.onReload(); } catch (err) { log.warn(...); }

onPluginSettingsPersist in http-routes.ts is:
  onPluginSettingsPersist: () => {
    savePluginSettings(state, { ...state.pluginSettings }).catch(() => {});
  }

onReload() calls performConfigReload → reloadCore. In reloadCore (reload.ts around line 313):
  const newPluginSettings = { ...config.settings };  // reads from disk!
  state.pluginSettings = newPluginSettings;  // OVERWRITES in-memory value!

Since savePluginSettings is fire-and-forget and config.settings is read from disk during reload, the disk may still have the OLD settings when reload runs. This causes state.pluginSettings to be overwritten with stale data, and the subsequent plugins.changed notification to the extension shows the OLD settings.

Changes:
In extension-handlers.ts handleConfigSetPluginSettings:
  - Change: `callbacks.onPluginSettingsPersist();` (fire-and-forget)
  - To: await an explicit savePluginSettings call. The McpCallbacks interface doesn't expose savePluginSettings directly, so the fix should be: change onPluginSettingsPersist to return Promise<void> and await it, OR import savePluginSettings directly in extension-handlers.ts, OR add an explicit async persist callback.

The simplest fix: change the McpCallbacks.onPluginSettingsPersist callback signature from `() => void` to `() => Promise<void>` and await it before onReload. Update the implementation in http-routes.ts createMcpCallbacks to return the promise.

In http-routes.ts createMcpCallbacks:
  Change:
    onPluginSettingsPersist: () => {
      savePluginSettings(state, { ...state.pluginSettings }).catch(() => {});
    }
  To:
    onPluginSettingsPersist: () => savePluginSettings(state, { ...state.pluginSettings }).catch(() => {})
    (returns a Promise)

  And in handleConfigSetPluginSettings:
    await callbacks.onPluginSettingsPersist();
    try { await callbacks.onReload(); }

Constraints:
- Do NOT change the McpCallbacks interface for onPluginLog, onReload, queryExtension, onToolConfigChanged, or onPluginPermissionsPersist — only onPluginSettingsPersist changes
- Do NOT change reloadCore to not overwrite state.pluginSettings — that would break other callers
- The plugins.changed notification and { ok: true } result must still be sent after onReload completes

userStories.title: Fix settings-reload race in HTTP /plugin-settings endpoint
userStories.description: In handlePluginSettings (http-routes.ts), the same race exists: savePluginSettings is called fire-and-forget and then performConfigReload overwrites state.pluginSettings from disk. Fix by awaiting the disk write before the reload.
acceptanceCriteria: In handlePluginSettings, savePluginSettings is awaited before performConfigReload is called
After the endpoint completes, state.pluginSettings contains the settings from the request body, not stale disk data

userStories.title: Route extension JSON-RPC responses to the requesting connection only
userStories.description: In extension-handlers.ts, sendJsonRpcError and most handler functions use sendToExtension (broadcast to all connections) to send JSON-RPC responses back to the extension. In multi-profile mode, this sends responses to all connected browser profiles. If two profiles use the same numeric request ID, each receives the other's response, corrupting the side panel state. Fix by routing responses to the specific connection that sent the request.
acceptanceCriteria: sendJsonRpcError accepts an optional connectionId parameter and uses sendToConnection when provided
handleConfigGetState routes its result to the requesting connection's connectionId
handleConfigSetToolPermission, handleConfigSetPluginPermission, handleConfigSetSkipPermissions each route their JSON-RPC result ({ ok: true }) to the requesting connection
handleConfigSetPluginSettings routes its JSON-RPC result to the requesting connection

handlePluginSearch, handlePluginInstall, handlePluginUpdateFromRegistry, handlePluginRemove, handlePluginRemoveBySpecifier, handlePluginCheckUpdates, handleFolderOpen each route their results to the requesting connection
The broadcast sendToExtension calls for plugins.changed notifications are NOT changed — those should still broadcast to all connections
handleTabStateChanged routes its JSON-RPC error to the requesting connection
userStories.notes:
Files:
- platform/mcp-server/src/extension-handlers.ts (add connectionId param to handlers and sendJsonRpcError)
- platform/mcp-server/src/extension-protocol.ts (pass senderConn?.connectionId to all affected handlers)

Context:
sendJsonRpcError (extension-handlers.ts line 114-116):
  const sendJsonRpcError = (state, id, code, message) => {
    sendToExtension(state, { jsonrpc: '2.0', error: { code, message }, id });
  };

sendToConnection already exists in extension-handlers.ts:
  const sendToConnection = (state, connectionId, msg) => {
    const conn = state.extensionConnections.get(connectionId);
    if (!conn) return false;
    conn.ws.send(JSON.stringify(msg));
    return true;
  };

In extension-protocol.ts, handleExtensionMessage receives senderWs and resolves:
  const senderConn = senderWs ? findConnectionByWs(state, senderWs) : getAnyConnection(state);

This senderConn is only passed to handleTabSyncAll and handleTabStateChanged. For all other handlers, senderConn is resolved but unused.

Changes needed:
1. In extension-handlers.ts:
   - Modify sendJsonRpcError signature: add optional `connectionId?: string` param
   - When connectionId is provided, use sendToConnection; otherwise fall back to sendToExtension
   - Add connectionId param to: handleConfigGetState, handleConfigSetToolPermission, handleConfigSetPluginPermission, handleConfigSetSkipPermissions, handleConfigSetPluginSettings, handlePluginSearch, handlePluginInstall, handlePluginUpdateFromRegistry, handlePluginRemove, handlePluginRemoveBySpecifier, handlePluginCheckUpdates, handleFolderOpen, handleTabStateChanged
   - In each handler, pass connectionId to sendJsonRpcError and to the sendToExtension calls for the direct result (NOT for plugins.changed notifications)
   - handleServerSelfUpdate: also accept connectionId and route the result/error

2. In extension-protocol.ts (handleExtensionMessage):
   - For each handler call site, pass senderConn?.connectionId as the last argument
   - Example change:
     From: handleConfigGetState(state, id);
     To:   handleConfigGetState(state, id, senderConn?.connectionId);

Critical constraint — do NOT change the plugins.changed notification calls:
  sendToExtension(state, { method: 'plugins.changed', params: ... });
  These MUST still broadcast to all connections because all side panels need to update their UI.

Critical constraint — do NOT change sendToExtension itself, only add the optional routing.

Handler-by-handler changes in extension-handlers.ts (send the JSON-RPC result/error only to the requesting connection):
- handleConfigGetState: the sendToExtension for { result: ..., id } → sendToConnection
- handleConfigSetToolPermission: the sendToExtension for { result: { ok: true }, id } → sendToConnection; keep sendToExtension for plugins.changed
- handleConfigSetPluginPermission: same pattern
- handleConfigSetSkipPermissions: same pattern  
- handleConfigSetPluginSettings: the sendToExtension for { result: { ok: true }, id } → sendToConnection; keep sendToExtension for plugins.changed
- handlePluginSearch: the sendToExtension for { result: { results }, id } → sendToConnection
- handlePluginInstall: the sendToExtension for { result, id } → sendToConnection; keep sendToExtension for plugins.changed
- handlePluginUpdateFromRegistry: same as install
- handlePluginRemove: same pattern
- handlePluginRemoveBySpecifier: same pattern
- handlePluginCheckUpdates: sendToExtension for { result, id } → sendToConnection
- handleFolderOpen: { result: { ok: true }, id } → sendToConnection
- handleServerSelfUpdate: { result/error, id } → sendToConnection
- handleTabStateChanged: already receives senderConn; pass senderConn?.connectionId to sendError/sendJsonRpcError

sendPluginManagementError also uses sendToExtension — add optional connectionId param there too.

Exported functions list that need updating (check extension-handlers.ts exports at the bottom).

userStories.title: Add type validation for non-url settings in HTTP /plugin-settings endpoint
userStories.description: The HTTP /plugin-settings endpoint (handlePluginSettings in http-routes.ts) only validates url-type settings fields. String, number, boolean, and select type fields are accepted without type checking. The WebSocket handler (handleConfigSetPluginSettings) validates all types. Fix the HTTP endpoint to match.
acceptanceCriteria: handlePluginSettings validates string-type fields reject non-string values
handlePluginSettings validates number-type fields reject non-number values
handlePluginSettings validates boolean-type fields reject non-boolean values
handlePluginSettings validates select-type fields reject values not in the options list
When validation fails, a 400 response with a descriptive error is returned
The validation logic matches what handleConfigSetPluginSettings already does in extension-handlers.ts
